title: Soft pneumatic actuators for pushing fingers into extension
authors: James V. McCall; Gregory D. Buckner; Derek G. Kamper
model_type: actuator geometry trade-off plus FEM airflow estimate
validation_type: mechanical jig force and passive bending tests
question_supported: Which soft finger actuation concepts are realistic for a simplified finger system?
why_opened: Direct finger-scale actuator paper with explicit force, compliance, and geometry trade-offs.
why_read_now: Current thesis task needs strong actuator exemplars tied to simulation-first design choices.
why_relevant: Provides actuator-side parameters and validation metrics that map cleanly to a simplified finger-actuator study.
screening_notes: Screen after reduced-order model papers; likely strong source for actuator assumptions.
key_takeaway: Rectangular palmar pneumatic chambers achieved useful extension torque with low passive resistance and fast cycling.
limitations: Still glove-oriented and includes FEM; not itself a full finger biomechanical model.
decision_impact: Could support actuator architecture narrowing and bench-top metric selection.

title: Assist-as-needed control of a soft rehabilitation robot for the finger using an interaction torque observer
authors: Alireza Besharati; Afshin Taghvaeipour; Ali Kamali E.; Mohammad Zareinejad
model_type: interaction torque observer and assist-as-needed control
validation_type: controller evaluation on soft finger robot
question_supported: How should controllability be evaluated in a simplified rehabilitation-relevant setup?
why_opened: Potentially useful controllability paper for the variable-stiffness thesis angle.
why_read_now: Recent finger-specific control paper that may sharpen controllability metrics before model implementation.
why_relevant: Relevant because it frames assistance using interaction torque rather than only position tracking.
screening_notes: Read if controllability metrics become central in current memo or decision work.
key_takeaway: A finger robot can adapt assistance using estimated interaction torque without direct patient model dependence.
limitations: More control-heavy than mechanism-heavy; may be secondary unless controllability becomes a lead question.
decision_impact: Could influence how controllability and user interaction are defined in the thesis.
next_action: screen abstract and results for usable controllability metrics
